title: centrocarpha aristata
intro: Centrocarpha aristata is a plant species in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as broomrape. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants, usually grasses. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Centrocarpha aristata can be used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, and can be prepared as an infusion or decoction for herbal remedies.
